title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with our authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi). This easy, flavorful recipe brings the taste of Turkey to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm, authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with mint and chili flakes.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of this traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, Mercimek Corbasi, a perfect meal for any day.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish staple, a comforting red lentil soup known for its vibrant color and rich, savory flavor. This traditional recipe combines simple ingredients like red lentils, tomatoes, and aromatic spices to create a creamy, wholesome dish. Often served with a drizzle of chili-infused butter and a squeeze of fresh lemon, it's a heartwarming meal perfect for any time of year.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Garlic and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor and color.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils, dried mint, sweet paprika, and cumin to the pot. Stir everything together well, ensuring the lentils are coated with the aromatics and spices. Cook for 1 minute.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend and Season
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until desired consistency is reached.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili-Mint Butter (Optional Topping)
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add the red pepper flakes (Pul Biber) and a pinch of dried mint. Sauté for 30 seconds, being careful not to burn the butter or spices.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with the prepared chili-mint butter. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the soup, as is traditional in Turkey.

tips: For an authentic flavor, use Turkish red pepper flakes (Pul Biber) for the topping; it has a unique mild heat and fruity aroma.
If you prefer a lighter soup, you can omit the butter topping or use less. For a richer flavor, use chicken broth instead of vegetable broth or water.

Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if needed to adjust consistency.
article:
Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup, is more than just a dish; it's a warm embrace in a bowl, a staple found on every Turkish table from bustling city restaurants to cozy family homes. This vibrant, creamy, and deeply flavorful soup holds a special place in Turkish cuisine, celebrated for its comforting properties, ease of preparation, and remarkable nutritional value. Whether served as a hearty breakfast starter, a light lunch, or a comforting dinner, Mercimek Corbasi is a testament to the simple elegance and rich flavors of traditional Turkish cooking. Its beautiful reddish-orange hue, often garnished with a swirl of mint-infused oil and a squeeze of lemon, makes it as appealing to the eye as it is to the palate.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi is a quintessential Turkish soup made primarily from red lentils. Its name, "mercimek," means lentil, and "corbasi" means soup. What sets this soup apart is its smooth, velvety texture and its distinctive savory, slightly tangy flavor profile, often enhanced by a generous sprinkle of dried mint and a hint of red pepper. Unlike many other lentil soups that might be chunky, Mercimek Corbasi is typically pureed to a creamy consistency, making it incredibly satisfying and easy to digest. It’s a dish that embodies the warmth and hospitality of Turkey, often served with a wedge of lemon to brighten its earthy notes and a side of fresh, crusty bread for dipping. Rich in protein, fiber, and essential nutrients, this soup isn't just delicious; it's incredibly healthy, making it a go-to choice for a nourishing meal any time of day.

## Ingredients You Need for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Crafting an authentic **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** requires a few key ingredients, most of which are readily available and simple to work with. The magic lies in the combination and the quality of these components:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Ensure they are split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily to create that signature creamy texture.
*   **Olive Oil:** A good quality extra virgin olive oil forms the base for sautéing the aromatics and contributes to the soup's rich flavor.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** Essential aromatics that provide depth and a foundational savory flavor.
*   **Tomato Paste (Domates Salçası):** Adds a concentrated tomato flavor and a beautiful reddish color.
*   **Red Pepper Paste (Biber Salçası - optional but recommended):** If you can find it, a little sweet or hot red pepper paste will add an authentic layer of flavor and color.
*   **Spices:** Dried mint (nane) is crucial for the authentic taste, along with cumin, paprika, and a pinch of dried chili flakes (pul biber) for a gentle warmth.
*   **Broth or Water:** Vegetable broth for a vegetarian/vegan option, or chicken broth for added richness. Water works perfectly fine too, allowing the lentil flavor to shine.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste, for seasoning.
*   **Lemon:** Fresh lemon wedges are a non-negotiable garnish, providing a bright, acidic counterpoint to the soup's richness.
*   **For the Nane Yağı (Mint Oil Drizzle):** Butter or olive oil, dried mint, and a pinch of red pepper flakes. This vibrant topping is what truly elevates the soup.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Mercimek Corbasi

Making a delicious **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a comforting bowl:

1.  **Rinse the Lentils:** Place 1 cup of red lentils in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any starch and ensures a clearer, better-tasting soup.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2-3 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Build the Flavor Base:** Stir in 1 tablespoon of tomato paste (and 1 teaspoon of red pepper paste if using) along with 1 teaspoon of dried mint, 1 teaspoon of cumin, 1/2 teaspoon of paprika, and a pinch of chili flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen the paste's flavor.
4.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in 6-7 cups of vegetable or chicken broth (or water). Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have mostly broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend to Smoothness:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it's completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be extremely careful with hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.
6.  **Season and Adjust:** Return the pot to low heat. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Let it simmer gently for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
7.  **Prepare the Nane Yağı (Mint Drizzle):** While the soup simmers, melt 2 tablespoons of butter (or olive oil for vegan) in a small pan over medium-low heat. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the fragrant mint-infused oil (nane yağı). Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, authentic Mercimek Corbasi is all about paying attention to a few key details:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Seriously, don't skip this step. Rinsing removes excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy and sometimes cause digestive discomfort. It also helps achieve that vibrant color.
*   **Toast the Spices:** Cooking the tomato/pepper paste and spices for a minute or two before adding the liquid deepens their flavor and releases their essential oils, adding complexity to your soup.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Nane Yağı:** The mint-infused butter or oil drizzle isn't just a garnish; it's an integral part of the flavor profile of **Turkish Red Lentil Soup**. The warmth of the butter/oil combined with the aromatic dried mint and chili flakes adds a burst of flavor and a beautiful visual appeal.
*   **Blend Until Velvety:** For that signature creamy texture, make sure you blend the soup until it's completely smooth. No chunks should remain.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving. You might need a little more salt, pepper, or even a tiny squeeze of lemon directly into the pot if you want to brighten the overall flavor.
*   **Let it Rest (Optional):** Like many soups, Mercimek Corbasi often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen.

## Serving Suggestions

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Traditionally, it's served hot, fresh off the stove, with a few essential accompaniments:

*   **Lemon Wedges:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice over each bowl is non-negotiable. It cuts through the richness, brightens the flavors, and adds a refreshing tang.
*   **Crusty Bread or Pita:** Perfect for dipping and soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup. Turkish bread (pide) is ideal.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** A sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or mint can add a lovely burst of freshness and color.
*   **Pickled Vegetables (Turşu):** In Turkey, it's common to serve this soup alongside a small plate of various pickled vegetables, which offer a delightful tangy crunch that complements the soup beautifully.
*   **Simple Salad:** A light green salad with a simple olive oil and lemon dressing makes a great side for a more complete meal.
*   **As a Starter:** Its light yet satisfying nature makes it an excellent first course for any Turkish meal.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi is perfect as is, there are a few ways you can adapt it to your preferences or what you have on hand:

*   **Make it Vegan/Vegetarian:** This soup is naturally vegetarian. Simply ensure you use vegetable broth or water instead of chicken broth, and opt for olive oil in the nane yağı instead of butter.
*   **Add Vegetables:** While not traditional for this specific soup, you can add finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils for extra nutrition and a slightly different texture. Just ensure they cook until very soft before blending.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of chili flakes (pul biber) or red pepper paste to control the heat. For a milder soup, omit the chili flakes entirely.
*   **Herbs:** Experiment with other dried herbs like oregano, though dried mint is truly the hallmark of this soup. Fresh mint or parsley can be stirred in at the end or used as a garnish.
*   **Thickening:** If you prefer a thicker soup and don't want to rely solely on the lentils, you can whisk a tablespoon of flour into the sautéed aromatics before adding the tomato paste and liquid. Cook for a minute to cook out the raw flour taste.
*   **Different Lentils:** While red lentils are crucial for the classic smooth texture, you could make a similar soup with brown or green lentils, though it would likely be chunkier and require a longer cooking time.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey. Its simple ingredients transform into a bowl of profound comfort and flavor, making it a beloved dish worldwide. Whether you're seeking a nutritious meal, a comforting classic, or an authentic taste of Turkish cuisine, this **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** is sure to delight your senses and warm your soul. Give it a try, and you'll quickly understand why it holds such a cherished place in so many hearts.
